subject: Make `collectNewCapabilities` return an empty Map
commit_message:
Make `collectNewCapabilities` return an empty Map

- Made `collectNewCapabilities` return an empty HashMap instead of a null value

Issue-ID: SDC-2924
Signed-off-by: Chris Andre <chris.andre@yoppworks.com>
Change-Id: I58c35135be5a2614c3daeafbd766d53f401f917b

too many "formatting", hard to find "real" change, I'd suggest ONLY format code changed by you, and not whole file.

> too many "formatting", hard to find "real" change, I'd suggest ONLY format code changed by you, and not whole file.

I initially wanted to put the formatting itself in a different commit but was told not to do it. Changing the formatting of only the code I modified would probably inconsistency in terms of coding style but if the community is good with files not respecting the code style, I'll yield.

I felt like delivering a commit, with 1-2 files including white space changes only, can create dozens commits without actual content in the git history. I also get the approach that it makes it hard to see the real change, once it's delivered in one commit with actual code changes.

I like the idea of reformatting everything at once though. 

I just wouldn't like to tire contributors with rebasing or solving conflicts, we have many pending commits right now. But, I think if we align it this one time, it'll be worth the temporal rebasing pain.
Let me merge as much as possible before doing that, it's a matter of couple of days (since Frankfurt issues came up again... sorry)

Thanks!
Ofir
